svg.title: Which LLMs stream and which do not
svg.text: Almost everyone streams today — but the API lets you turn it off
① All major LLMs support streaming — and default to it in their chat UIs
② Any LLM API lets you turn streaming OFF — you just get the full response at the end
{"{"} "stream": true {"}"} → tokens arrive one by one as they are generated
{"{"} "stream": false {"}"} → wait for all tokens, get one big JSON at the end
③ When do developers actually use non-streaming (stream: false)?
④ One real exception — "thinking" / reasoning models do something different
OpenAI o1 / o3 · Google Gemini Thinking · Claude Extended Thinking
These models think silently first — running thousands of internal tokens you never see.
The visible stream only starts once the thinking is done.
So you see a long pause → then streaming begins. Still SSE, but with a delay.
